message:
feat(protocol,runtime,conformance): distinguish unsupported RESP3 types from truly invalid prefixes

Previously, the RESP parser treated all unrecognized type prefix bytes
uniformly as `InvalidPrefix`, producing a generic "invalid RESP type
prefix" error regardless of whether the byte was a known RESP3 type
identifier or a completely unknown byte. This made it impossible for
operators and threat-detection pipelines to distinguish between a
client legitimately attempting RESP3 (which FrankenRedis does not
support) and a client sending garbage bytes on the wire.

This change introduces a new `UnsupportedResp3Type(u8)` variant to
`RespParseError` that explicitly matches all ten RESP3 type prefix
bytes: `~` (set), `%` (map), `#` (boolean), `,` (double), `_` (null),
`(` (big number), `=` (verbatim string), `|` (attribute), `>` (push),
and `!` (blob error). When one of these prefixes is encountered, the
parser now returns `UnsupportedResp3Type` instead of `InvalidPrefix`,
and the runtime maps it to a distinct error string:
"ERR Protocol error: unsupported RESP3 type prefix '<char>'"
rather than the generic "invalid RESP type prefix '<char>'" message.
Truly unknown bytes (e.g. `?`) continue to produce `InvalidPrefix`.

Changes by crate:

  fr-protocol/src/lib.rs:
  - Added `UnsupportedResp3Type(u8)` variant to `RespParseError` enum.
  - Implemented `Display` for the new variant with a dedicated message.
  - Added a match arm in `parse_frame_internal` that catches all ten
    RESP3 prefix bytes and returns `UnsupportedResp3Type`.
  - Added three unit tests: one for a RESP3 set prefix (`~`), one
    confirming unknown prefixes still error as `InvalidPrefix`, and
    one exhaustive test iterating over all ten RESP3 prefix bytes.

  fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:
  - Extended `protocol_error_to_resp` to map `UnsupportedResp3Type`
    to the new "unsupported RESP3 type prefix" error string.
  - Added integration test `protocol_unsupported_resp3_type_error_string`
    verifying the full round-trip: raw bytes -> runtime -> RESP error
    frame, including threat event assertions (ParserAbuse, S0 severity,
    FailClosed decision, protocol_parse_failure reason code).

  fr-conformance/fixtures/protocol_negative.json:
  - Renamed `invalid_prefix` test case to `unsupported_resp3_prefix`
    and updated its expected error string to match the new wording.
  - Added `unsupported_resp3_map_prefix` test case for the `%` prefix.
  - Added `invalid_prefix_unknown_byte` test case for the `?` prefix
    to ensure truly unknown bytes still produce the original error.

All three new conformance fixtures include full `expect_threat` blocks
asserting parser_abuse / s0 / fail_closed classification, consistent
with the existing negative-protocol threat contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

       }
     },
     {
-      "name": "invalid_prefix",
+      "name": "unsupported_resp3_prefix",
       "now_ms": 0,
       "raw_request": "~1\r\n",
-      "expect": { "kind": "error", "value": "ERR Protocol error: invalid RESP type prefix '~'" },
+      "expect": { "kind": "error", "value": "ERR Protocol error: unsupported RESP3 type prefix '~'" },
+      "expect_threat": {
+        "threat_class": "parser_abuse",
+        "severity": "s0",
+        "decision_action": "fail_closed",
+        "reason_code": "protocol_parse_failure",
+        "subsystem": "protocol",
+        "action": "parse_failure"
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "unsupported_resp3_map_prefix",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "raw_request": "%1\r\n",
+      "expect": { "kind": "error", "value": "ERR Protocol error: unsupported RESP3 type prefix '%'" },
+      "expect_threat": {
+        "threat_class": "parser_abuse",
+        "severity": "s0",
+        "decision_action": "fail_closed",
+        "reason_code": "protocol_parse_failure",
+        "subsystem": "protocol",
+        "action": "parse_failure"
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "invalid_prefix_unknown_byte",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "raw_request": "?\r\n",
+      "expect": { "kind": "error", "value": "ERR Protocol error: invalid RESP type prefix '?'" },
       "expect_threat": {
         "threat_class": "parser_abuse",
         "severity": "s0",
